content:
✨ seeker | CSS
🎯 Primary Use Case: To demonstrate and educate users about how malicious websites can exploit location permissions and gather sensitive device information through social engineering, acting as a proof-of-concept for security awareness.
✨ Key Features: • Hosts fake websites to request location permissions
📖 Summary: Seeker is a tool designed to accurately locate smartphones by hosting a fake website that requests location permissions. Upon user consent, it gathers precise location data (longitude, latitude, accuracy, altitude) and device information (unique ID, model, OS, hardware specs, IP addresses) through social engineering tactics. It serves as a proof-of-concept to educate users about potential data privacy risks from malicious websites.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
